programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The co-pilot of a plane carrying Dr Sloan to a conference is murdered by persons unknown mid-flight, and the remainder of the crew members find themselves struck down by a mystery illness. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An adulterous woman kills her wealthy husband, and then tries to convince the authorities his death was caused by a medical condition, implicating Dr Sloan in the process. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble discovers that negligence caused the death of a salvage diver and jeopardises his own life when he befriends the girl accused of being a jinx

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: James Kincannon finds himself in need of Perry's help after not only being accused of jewel fencing, but also pushing a man out of a penthouse window

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team tries to provide the evidence needed to convict a serial killer but faces a series of challenges and struggles to reach the courtroom in time

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A police officer is murdered after stopping a driver for routine questioning, and Calleigh discovers evidence of underhand dealings by the victim, as well as disturbing links to a carjacking ring and a former felon. However, as the net closes, the case begins to fall apart

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble is wounded by a deputy's bullet and finds refuge in an Indian school, but not for long

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: When an embezzler returns from prison, a woman thought to be involved in his crime demands that he clear her name, but is soon accused of his murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. Meanwhile, Gibbs continues to worry about Shepard's health, convinced she and Ducky are hiding something. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's two-star rookies must track down a terrorist who threatens to unleash a deadly bioweapon on the city if the authorities do not meet his demands within 48 hours

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. Jenny Shepard is the prime suspect, as she believed the criminal killed her father some years earlier, so Gibbs forms a plan to help his boss. However, an eyewitness later comes forward and implicates Tony. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As the Serious Organised Crime Agency begin to question Zain's undercover role with the Colombian gang, drugs mule Eva Garcia contacts her brother, blowing the security of the safe house and putting the detective in great danger from kingpin Alvarez. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tensions are running high as the day of Baz's funeral dawns, and despite Charlie and Dan's best efforts to keep their antipathy under control for Louis's sake, it seems inevitable that strong feelings will boil over. Meanwhile, Lara decides to risk everything and challenge Harry, while Jim receives shocking news about Andy

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack and Soca Officer Marcus Swift follow the false lead and prepare to corner the drugs gang at Jamaica Dock, while newcomer Honey is being held prisoner at Bonham Wharf boatyard, hoping in vain for help from her colleagues. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's a busy day for Dr Legg as a mystery virus spreads. Cindy crashes into a car after seeing Sam and David hugging

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie asks Peggy about dating. Ian is determined to win the pub quiz. Bianca has food poisoning but David and Ricky argue about who should look after her

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth decides to organise a formal party to demonstrate how out of her depth Sarah is. Meanwhile, Carolyn is shocked by Jack's revelation when she challenges him to confront his intimacy issues, and Regina returns home from Europe with news about Sarah. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will comes to the aid of a feuding couple, helping them to see that life's too short for petty squabbles, while Alex has to summon up the courage of her convictions as a Down's syndrome patient presents her with a moral dilemma. Simon Shepherd and Maggie O'Neill star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James visits the dog track dash, but the greyhound owners consider him a touch too conscientious when his services are called upon. Tristan stirs up trouble when he tries his hand at cooking. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey adds another string to his bow by filling in for the local vicar. Campsite comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Jeffrey Holland, Ruth Madoc and Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The day of the annual Yellowcoat competition dawns - and the stakes are raised when a Caribbean holiday is up for grabs. Comedy, starring Su Pollard and Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth discovers Emmet has planned a piano concert for the senior citizens at the church hall, and decides to treat the lucky audience to her own brand of sing-song. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and David Griffin

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Chaos abounds at an annual parade held to celebrate a famous battle. The lifelike effigy of the old General has been spruced up and Nora Batty prepares to sit on a float playing the part of his wife. Norman Wisdom makes a cameo appearance

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered sleuths are hired to work behind the scenes of a garden makeover TV show, but Rosemary has an accident while standing in for the programme's star and ends up in hospital. Realising someone is trying to sabotage the series, Laura teams up with no-nonsense presenter Quinnie Dorell (Belinda Lang) to catch the culprit. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: The private detective tells the police that the circumstances of Rochelle's death merit further investigation, but is dismissed. He then seeks out Lula's friends and learns that she was looking for her biological family, before visiting the Bristow family home and meeting Lady Yvette, who reveals that she and John were watching a film when Lula died. Meanwhile, more of the Strike's personal history is revealed to Robin when his half-sister Lucy invades the office

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Sasha is occupied with a training course, the boys' investigations take them to a lookalike agency, where their top two acts were found dead in an apparent double suicide 13 years ago. In an industry reliant on the art of the illusion and their suspects intent on giving them the run around, Ted, Steve and Danny are convinced there's more to this case than first meets the eye. Steve is thrilled when their enquiries take them to the door of one of his football heroes, but his glee is short-lived. Detective drama, starring Denis Lawson

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The failure of another attempt to limit Iran's nuclear threat proves all the other nations have their own agendas and operate by their own sets of rules. Adam kills a mugger who turns out to be a journalist investigating the Tehran bombing, and his day gets even worse when it becomes clear Ana has been compromised. Meanwhile, Yalta tests Ros's loyalties further

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack and Soca Officer Marcus Swift follow the false lead and prepare to corner the drugs gang at Jamaica Dock, while newcomer Honey is being held prisoner at Bonham Wharf boatyard, hoping in vain for help from her colleagues. Haskew finally convinces Kristen that Zain really is an undercover cop, and Jack is horrified to learn his detective has been sleeping with the sultry club owner

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Change is on the way to the sleepy market town of Cranford as the railway pushes out from Manchester, but many of the female residents are more excited about the arrival of the handsome new doctor, Frank Harrison. Adapted by Heidi Thomas, with an all-star cast including Judi Dench and Eileen Atkins, who was awarded a Bafta for her role as the authoritative Miss Deborah Jenkyns

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Dr Harrison's plans to become better acquainted with Sophy are thwarted by Caroline Tomkinson and Clara Smith, Mr Carter takes pity on an illiterate 10-year-old and Miss Matty is shaken to see a face from her past. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins

Charlie presents Dan with a court order to stop him taking Louis out of the country

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Friday night shift proves a hectic time for the Sun Hill coppers as a childhood friend of Amber's is raped, a drug-dealer is collared and a mugging victim is attacked for a second time - and in the aftermath of all the excitement, Phil's unruly hormones are fired by an encounter with a fellow sex addict. A guilt-ridden Honey decides to tell Steve their relationship is over, Neil pours out his heart to Andrea, and Yvonne learns she's no longer Acting Sergeant - leading to much speculation about her replacement

programme.desc: Michelle gets a surprise at work while Nigel braves out Debbie's inquest. Alan and Carol find a safe haven

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will makes the professional mistake of getting emotionally attached to a patient with psychiatric problems, and a black cloud descends over Kerri and Sam's engagement party. Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, John Duttine and Denise Black

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried investigates when an outbreak of dog poisoning sweeps through the district, while Tristan joins the local bellringers for a farewell drink. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin demotes Gladys and replaces her with a new supervisor, who requires her own chalet - throwing the accommodation situation into chaos. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Barry and Yvonne tread the light fantastic, and Spike has a humorous conversation with Pinocchio, while Ted attempts to foil Joe Maplin's expansion plans. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daddy dresses up in a spaceman suit and goes on the rampage in the toy department of a local shop, forcing an embarrassed Hyacinth to intervene. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lionel has a habit of being unpredictable, but when he walks off and abandons his wife while out on a drive in the hills, it is left to Clegg, Truly and Billy to console her. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Roy Hudd guest stars

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the mystery drama. The sleuthing senior citizen is urged by an old friend to visit her sister as she is convinced something is wrong. Miss Marple heads for the country to investigate and it is not long before a heated argument in a locked room ends with a gunshot that leaves a man dead. Jean Simmons and Joss Ackland guest star, with Joan Hickson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: When Ros's bugs inform Yalta that Harry intends to ground all planes to prevent a nuclear warhead circuit board reaching Iran, she and Adam go undercover on an airline flying to the country intending to identify the courier. Meanwhile, Jo and Malcolm must stop the poisoning of London's water supply, as threatened by terrorists seeking to guarantee that Iran has nuclear capability

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part two of two. Lara dives underwater to save Simon and manages to resuscitate him, but he has no sensation in his legs - and it's not long before the finger of blame is pointed at Harry. Abs faces a moral dilemma when a convicted murderer is brought in for treatment and wants her medication increased to a lethal dose, while an influx of patients causes mayhem in the department

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gina reports Honey as a missing person when she fails to report for work and it's not long before her fingerprints are found at Bonham Wharf, identifying her as the second prisoner at the scene. Zain panics as the net closes on him, but Kristen remains calm and reassures him they can make a clean getaway. John Heaton's wife tells him they are through and Emma finds out just how controlling her new husband can be

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky lends Robbie a sympathetic ear. Gita and Sharmilla wave goodbye to Sanjay, and the Jacksons are on the up, but it's more bad news for Michelle

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Kathy is slowly facing up to her condition, but an evening with Pat reawakens old memories. Michelle opens the door to an unexpected visitor - and an unexpected offer of a job in USA

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah arrives in Paris, hoping to reconnect with her husband, and is shocked by what she finds. Elizabeth pushes George to be truthful, but causes more damage to their already fragile relationship. Regina puts the next stage of her plan into action, and while Anna finds out the truth about her real father, Olivia proves the depths she is prepared to go to in order to save her marriage

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: The Beeches partnership is suddenly thrown into a state of turmoil as Sam and Kerri are seriously injured in a road crash on a remote country lane, leaving them fighting for their lives. Will and Alex struggle to keep a professional distance as they battle to save them, while Tom helps a sick girl. Joseph Millson and Susannah Corbett star.

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James makes an eye-opening call to Lord Hulton to deal with a runaway horse, and his failure to save a dog brings shocking consequences. Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin's plans for expansion cause chaos with the Yellowcoats when an old cottage is scheduled for demolition. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The Yellowcoats fight to save Fred's horses from the glue factory - with unexpected results. Comedy, starring Felix Bowness

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is waiting for a new three-piece suite to be delivered and goes to extraordinary lengths to ensure her neighbours see the royal warrant on the delivery van. But, as ever, her family contrives to take the gloss off her moment of glory. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Billy celebrates his ancestor Robin Hood's birthday by proudly wearing a green suit, and gullible Barry pays tribute in his own way - much to the amusement of everyone else. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Keith Clifford and Mike Grady star

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley faces charges of intimidating a suspect, and is suspended from duty, meaning Havers and new partner DI Fiona Knight (guest star Liza Tarbuck) must investigate when the body of a woman is found in a lake. However, the detective involves himself in the case, compromising his chances of an unbiased trial. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A skeleton uncovered in the foundations of a swimming pool is identified as a young lottery winner named Cheryl, who was reported missing 17 years ago. The prime suspects are her fellow quiz team-mates, who won their lucky windfall as part of a syndicate, and while some have put their money to good use, the victim's boyfriend has fallen on hard times and the media remain convinced he is the key to her murder. As allegations of affairs, fights and betrayal begin to surface, the team is left to ponder whether money really is the root of all evil

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Terrorists hijack a live TV broadcast that should have heralded the signing of a new peace agreement with Iran, and Malcolm must try to disable the signal while allowing the infiltrators to believe they are still on air. As Adam and Ros try to defuse the situation in the studio, a shot rings out leading to secrets and lies from all sides being revealed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gina reports Honey as a missing person when she fails to report for work and it's not long before her fingerprints are found at Bonham Wharf, identifying her as the second prisoner at the scene. Zain panics as the net closes on him, but Kristen remains calm and reassures him they can make a clean getaway. John Heaton's wife tells him they are through and Emma finds out just how controlling her new husband can be

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The true nature of friendship within Cranford is revealed as the ladies rally to help Miss Matty when she receives terrible news. Meanwhile, Dr Harrison suffers as his career, reputation and relationship all seem ruined, while Miss Galindo and Mr Carter grow closer as they face up to the tragedy of Hanbury. Judi Dench, Philip Glenister and Simon Woods star

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: First in a two-part special. The story picks up in August 1844, two years after Miss Matty lost her beloved sister Deborah. Mr Buxton has returned to live quietly in Cranford following the death of his wife, although Miss Matty is concerned about his relationship with his son as neither seem to be coping with their bereavement. The local busybody is also worried about Peggy Bell, who lives in an isolated cottage looking after her inattentive mother and domineering brother, so she hatches a plot to bring these young residents together. However, the surprise arrival of Lady Ludlow's son Lord Septimus leads to a chain of unexpected events concerning the looming railway, and a tragedy at Matty's own house forces her to re-evaluate her opinions on the subject. Judi Dench and Jonathan Pryce star

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Margie Cooper start their hunt in the west of Scotland, buying items including a model of a Scottish terrier and a a Robert Burns-themed object to sell at auction in Dumfries

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat feels nervous about the robbery and with tensions escalating between her and Shirley, she tells the Mitchells the job is off. Can Ben convince her to go through with it? Denise tries to find the courage to tell Raymond she is his birth mother, aware it will come as a shock to the youngster - but she is the one in for a surprise. Tina discovers she has outstayed her welcome

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, TV presenters Gabby Logan and Melvin Odoom and Cold Feet star Fay Ripley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The barrister helps Noemie Lopian find out how her French mother survived the war after she was arrested as a child by the Nazis, meeting the grandson of the man who hid her mother and siblings in a chicken shed in his garden during round-ups of Jews in the town of Saint-Junien. Noemie also learns of the incredible sacrifice and courage of a young female French resistance fighter who saved her mother's life

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The salon juniors find out which of them are getting promoted, things step up between Sammyjo and her crush, and there is surprise for Bev as one Longlox girl has some big news

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Filmed across four days as the Covid-19 pandemic develops, Sammyjo and Bev deal with professional and personal challenges leading up to their biggest decision yet

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, married couple Dan and Alison from Swansea face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves to be the undoing of a con man claiming he cannot work due to an injury, and in America, a doctor abuses his position to commit medical health insurance fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A doctor and a paramedic try to save the life of a hiker who fell 650ft, while a motorist takes evasive action when a car heads towards him on the wrong side of the road

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma's suspension hits the local press with an unexpected headline, the Pitman Telegraph is put to the test, and a test result turns the life of a Baking Vlogger upside down

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson and Ginny Buckley catch up with an adventurous couple with three huskies who relocated to Derbyshire and lifelong city dwellers who moved to the West Sussex coast

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph gets creative with a mannequin at Earlswood Recycling Centre, while restorers Neil Wragg and Rachel Henderson get to work on some sofa leather and an old metal trunk

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp hit the antique shops of Scotland, where they find an unusual pyjama case and a rare piece of Victoriana to sell at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben, Kush and Phil head to the job, while Shirley and Kat create a diversion and act as lookout. Can they pull it off? Callum is caught between a rock and a hard place, while Max has an idea to help Linda, making Sharon suspicious. With nowhere to go, Tina asks Kathy if she can stay with her, while Suki offers Ian a deal

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac rushes in a mystery patient, whose arrival sets in motion in a deepening medical crisis that could have serious repercussions for her secret relationship with Kian. Keller is shocked when a new member of staff reveals the truth about Sacha's deception, and is tempted to take out his anguish on a vulnerable patient. Cameron basks in the glory of being declared a hero - prompting Nicky to issue him with an ultimatum

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When calls start coming in to report debris on the M4, police discover that a father and son have been in a serious crash. As traffic chaos builds, the pressure is on for the collision team to find out why the car left the road

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Marcus Waring challenges two of today's four chefs to butcher a chicken, debone the thighs and cook them with asparagus and a brandy, mushroom and mustard cream sauce, all in just 20 minutes. The others are set a task by Monica Galetti, and must create a fruit tart filled with a berry compote, piped pistachio chantilly and candied pistachios

programme.title: Ovie: Life After Reality TV
programme.desc: Professional basketball player and Love Island star Ovie Soko decides which direction to take his life, as he explores the good and the bad of the reality TV industry.

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The barrister helps Noemie Lopian find out how her French mother survived the war after she was arrested as a child by the Nazis, meeting the grandson of the man who hid her mother and siblings in a chicken shed in his garden during round-ups of Jews in the town of Saint-Junien. Noemie also learns of the incredible sacrifice and courage of a young female French resistance fighter who saved her mother's life

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development of a commercial property in Ebbw Vale in Blaenau Gwent, as well as semi-detached houses in Lower Gornal in the West Midlands and Gillingham in Kent

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A holiday insurance scam is foiled when investigators uncover forged documents, while CCTV footage exposes the false claims of a man who said he was hit by a bus

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A boy taking part in a karting race is sent flying into the air as his father watches from the stands, and a man who dived into a river to save a dog needs rescuing as well

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents the programme from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assisting the reds and blues

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Lily has a big surprise for Al, and Daniel and Zara get some upsetting news. Meanwhile, Rob uncovers a disturbing truth

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah revisits two sets of Escape to the Country house-hunters united by their appreciation of traditional, period homes. In Wiltshire, she catches up with a couple for whom it was love at first sight with picture-perfect character cottages shown to them on their 2019 house hunt. Heading to Hampshire, Sonali meets another buyer who succeeded in finding her dream home - a property filled with positive energy where she could practice meditation in a serene setting

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues a broken-up sideboard, two rattan chairs and wooden bed ends from the recycling centre, hoping she and her expert friends can turn them into money-making items

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp search antiques in the Lake District, where Natasha falls for a rare piece of Edwardian silver

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Barack Obama Talks to David Olusoga
programme.desc: Historian David Olusoga is joined in conversation by former American President Barack Obama to discuss his long awaited memoir, A Promised Land. He also reflects on the volatile racial divide in the US, his steadfast refusal to abandon American ideals, and revealing details of life as a black First Family in the White House

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. Sue Nunn from Chichester is hoping wood restorer Will Kirk can bring her late husband's fishing tackle box back to its former glory. Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch have a huge task on their hands when they care for a doll with an extraordinary story. Margaret Dyson's beloved doll, Alice, was badly damaged in 1945 when an RAF plane crash landed into her family home, tragically killing her mother

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Four more professional chefs arrive from across the nation to compete, first taking on the infamous Skills Test and recreating dishes set by Marcus and Monica. The first two chefs must French trim a rack of Lamb and cook a portion served with a mint pesto, with the second pair of competitors asked to fillet a plaice and cook one portion, and serve with a chicken butter sauce and pickled radish. In the second round, the four chefs must impress the judges with two stand out Signature Dishes of their own creation, before two of the competitors are sent home

programme.title: The Rap Game UK
programme.desc: Under the watchful eye of DJ Target, Krept & Konan, the artists perform club tunes to Ms Banks, listening from another room and judging their tracks purely based on sound alone

programme.title: Step into the Ring
programme.desc: Documentary following aspiring young wrestlers from Norwich, with gym owner, trainer and promoter Zak Knight on hand to help them every step of the way

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson returns to the Isle of Wight, the location of a previous property hunt, wanting to discover whether a couple he helped succeeded in finding a balance of home and business potential in their new setting. Back on the mainland, Jules travels to south-west Wales to meet a husband and wife who craved a sizable Welsh farmhouse, but who have found that a home is about so much more than bricks and mortar

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Presenter Jay Blades enlists the help of designer Sarah Peterson to give two side tables a stylish new look, while lighting expert Duncan McKean attempts to give two old record players a radical overhaul. For his own project, Jay attempts to make some money for nothing from two farmhouse stools. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir is racing to an urgent call-out from a local pedigree sheep breeder, whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, finding her only choice is to put the calf down or take on a challenging amputation. Meanwhile, Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the opening episode, Mary heads back to the city where she learnt to cook, Paris, where she visits one of the city's creperies to finesse her technique. Later, inspired by pastries in Parisian patisserie windows, she makes her own brioche and apple frangipane tart

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: It is the eve of Woody and Tracy's wedding, so not a great time for a power cut across the whole town, but everyone pulls together to make it a day to remember

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter K. Highlights include Rick Stein cooking lamb koftas and Raymond Blanc serving up kougelhopf

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day two from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Eventual champion Stefanos Tsitsipas got off to a winning start in this session last year, prevailing 7-6, 6-4 against Daniil Medvedev

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores nature's evolutionary anomalies, beginning by examining how the unique features of the chameleon and the giraffe have benefited them in their respective environments. He then looks at curious creatures that have led to accusations of forgery, such as the duck-billed platypus and the midwife toad

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, as well as new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin uncovers the history of the border town Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the third couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of he Weimar Republic, Alex Higgins, the bridges of London, and UK cheeses, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as two teams who lost their first-round matches get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising Black suits, White meat, Blue corner, and Green light

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: It is mid-summer and the Royal Free London NHS Hospital Foundation Trust faces the long-lasting effects of Covid-19, with departments across the trust battling for scarce theatre slots while trying to prioritise their volume of cases. At Barnet Hospital, a large team meeting is underway when a clinician is informed he has tested positive for coronavirus and must leave the site immediately, while the infection control team springs into action to identify who has had 'significant contact' with him and must therefore also go home and isolate for 14 days

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Clara Amfo and Dev Griffin to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the first series of Strictly Come Dancing and the final episodes of Friends and Sex and the City

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: World Cup 1966: Alfie's Boys
programme.desc: David Jason presents a documentary about how Alf Ramsey recruited the England football team that won the world cup in 1966, and was able to unite the members into a world class side. Featuring archive footage of the team in action and contributions from Bobby Charlton, Jack Charlton, Jimmy Greaves, George Cohen, Tina Moore, Harry Redknapp, Terry Venables, Geoff Hurst and Gordon Banks

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Incident Response Officer Andy is dispatched when a call comes in about a reported stabbing, while Adam and Orani are dispatched to treat a long-term heroin addict whose concerned mother has called the ambulance service for help. Elsewhere, Kieran and Mick are dispatched to their second patient of the night, an 82-year-old lady who has fallen at home and has been waiting for an ambulance for two hours

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: The team tracks the changes in nature throughout autumn, with Chris Packham in the New Forest watching how it transforms the area near his home, while Michaela Strachan is at a NatureScot reserve in Fife to report on a giant grey seal breeding colony on the nearby Isle of May

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson catches up with a couple who bought a home in North Yorkshire, before Nicki Chapman enjoys a reunion with a couple who were seeking a property in the Welsh Borders

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, then she and two experts transform a nursing chair, a coffee table and some vintage bottles

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets the army of people working to meet the country's energy demands. She begins 75 miles off the Yorkshire coast at the world's biggest offshore windfarm. Hornsea One has broken every record for its size and scale - with 10,000 people involved in its construction in the North Sea. Keeley meets some of the staff who made it happen - but can she get to the top of one of the 174 turbines in the field?

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Warrington Wolves, Hull FC v Wigan Warriors and Huddersfield Giants v Catalans Dragons

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and Anna Haugh prepare George V's favourite curry, Polly Russell discovers how Francis Drake solved a debt crisis and the secrets of a famous condiment are revealed

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day three from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Six-time champion Roger Federer overcame Matteo Berrettini 7-6, 6-3 in this session last year, but eventually failed to qualify for the final

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, revealing how these entirely different creatures have both adapted to life in the dark. He also looks at gorillas and vampire bats both gained unpleasant reputations within the animal kingdom

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall in Suffolk, where they uncover a 17th-century apothecary jar, while Paul Martin learns about a medieval port lost to the sea

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball chats to more couples about their training progress. Plus, a closer look at the weekend's performances, as well as more backstage gossip

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Russell Kane and presenter AJ Odudu navigate their way around Shropshire looking for antiques, accompanied by experts Phil Serrell and Margie Cooper. Eventually they head to an auction to discover whose haul raises the most money

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers come to the aid of a family trapped in their car on the causeway connecting the Holy Island of Lindisfarne to the mainland, who face being slowly engulfed by the sea. The crew of one of the RNLI's few inland stations in Ireland assists two capsized kayakers just a few days after Christmas, and on Dorset's Jurassic Coast, a fossil hunter is cut off by a rising tide

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the comedy panel game, in which Sandi Toksvig takes on the subject of travel, accompanied by Alan Davies and their guests

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: The company tries to move on in the wake of the recent tragedy. Yasmin's relationship with her boyfriend hits rock bottom, but flirting with Robert and a surprising game of cat and mouse provide a welcome distraction. Harper is desperate to get out of her temporary accommodation and away from the bad memories - and receives help from a surprising source

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara's father Lewis is back in her life, and his return forces her to re-examine all her important male relationships. She tries to dig deeper into why Lewis and her fiancé both left her. Guest starring Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: It is dinner at Trent Manor, and everyone around the table has a motive for murdering Lord Trent. But who did? Mandy wasn't invited to the manor, so she is off doing something else. Comedy short, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with guest star David Bradley

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Life in Lockdown: A Special Film for BBC Children in Need
programme.desc: A powerful, uplifting and touching film which proves how brilliantly resilient children and young people can be in the face of a global crisis. This film shines a light on children and young people experiencing a variety of challenges whilst they also cope with the effects of the pandemic, and reveals how BBC Children in Need funded projects are providing vital support for families during this incredibly difficult time. Narrated by Emma Willis

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill focuses on the history of home improvement shows. From Do It Yourself with Barry Bucknell to Grand Designs, Harry reveals the tricks of the trade when making these shows

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams all look at the unseen side of autumn from their four separate locations across the country. Tonight, Michaela shares more drama from the Scottish seal colony, and Chris reveals the latest antics of the multi-coloured badger family

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph gets creative with a mannequin at Earlswood Recycling Centre, while restorers Neil Wragg and Rachel Henderson get to work on some sofa leather and an old metal trunk

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Liz Carr traces her family tree, learning about the life of her maternal grandfather, who died when her mother was just two. She also discovers that her paternal great-great-great-grandfather was involved in the attempted murder of a prominent landlord in Armagh in the 1850s

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: After his eventful evening at Woody and Tracy's wedding, George tries once and for all to sort out his love life without upsetting anyone. Erik Thomson stars

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter R, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day four from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Rafael Nadal came from a set down to defeat Daniil Medvedev 6-7, 6-3, 7-6 in this session last year, but a third title in this event was to elude him

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist reflects on the remarkable abilities that some plants and animals possess, such as the electric eel that helped inspire the battery and flowers that 'talk' to bees using electrical signals. Early naturalists were puzzled as to where some animals disappeared to each year and the discovery of the swallow's epic migration and the butterfly's magical transformation were scientific stories once cloaked in mystery and controversy

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion and Nick Davies are at Huddersfield Town Hall to find items to take to auction, while Paul Martin finds out about former prime minister Harold Wilson

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball gives all the backstage gossip and exclusive news from Strictly Come Dancing, and Ian Waite gives his thoughts on the couples' training progress

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anneka Rice and Liz Carr head out on a treasure-hunting quest in the Midlands, joined by expert assistants Philip Serrell and James Braxton, before going to auction in Greenwich. Liz also learns how criminal investigations were brought into the modern age, while Anneka heads to a local farm - much to the bewilderment of the farmer

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of first-time puppy owners. Rescue dog Elina moves in with the Orchard family in Buckinghamshire after a 2,000-mile trip from Cyprus, where she was found abandoned on the side of the road. Meanwhile, Sage the Staffordshire bull terrier cross is a massive hit with the Fianko family

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. The Pinchers turn to blackmail to get what they want. Meanwhile, Lydia persuades the Marquess of Blayne to give Kate another chance with Prince Henry. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedians Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson parody a 1980s safety video

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A special compilation show of great stand-up, taken from over 15 series, showcasing all the funniest bits from the disabled stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. Featuring Adam Hills, Rosie Jones, Lost Voice Guy, Francesca Martinez, Chris McCausland, Jack Carroll, Tanyalee Davis and Jonny Pelham

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Comedians perform a selection of quickfire sketches, songs and isolation anecdotes, filmed by themselves in and around their own homes during the coronavirus lockdown. In the opening instalment, Bob Mortimer's Train Guy comes to life in an animation, Michael Spicer takes on Priti Patel in his viral hit Room Next Door and married comedians Rachel Parris and Marcus Brigstocke go up against each other in a lip sync battle

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Documentary following the day-to-day realities the NHS faces in unprecedented times, focusing on the issues and challenges that continually dominate the headlines

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The crew of a Weston-super-Mare lifeboat race to the aid of three casualties who have been caught by a rising tide and are exhausted from trying to swim against the current. On the northeast coast of England, volunteers come to the aid of a father and son trapped against a cliff face, while in Swanage, two lifeboats are launched to attend to a grounded yacht and a badly injured sailor

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness introduces Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff to his latest creation - an all-electric, all-terrain ice-cream van for the 21st century. Elsewhere, the team tests the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric with a race around a deserted Alton Towers

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham catches up with white-tailed eagles released on the Isle of Wight, and shares the surprising science that reveals exactly why getting out into nature is good for you

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred uncovers the construction techniques of stonemasons and carpenters through the ages, focusing on Norman cathedrals. The former steeplejack admires Peterborough Cathedral and Rochester Castle, where he demonstrates how tactics used to invade the fortress centuries ago resemble today's demolition methods

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a majestic stand of evergreen trees overlooking sweet tiny flowers growing on a sloping knoll

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Social historian Emma Dabiri and Dr Bendor Grosvenor travel to Glasgow to investigate a long-lost picture of George Villiers, Duke of Buckingham, who was the lover of James VI of Scotland, possibly painted by one of the most famous artists in history

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the dramatic story of the devastating fire at the Paris cathedral on April 15, 2019, following the firefighters who faced the responsibility of saving one of the city's most emblematic and much-loved symbols from burning to the ground

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre Dame: Inside the Great Cathedral Rescue
programme.desc: Following efforts to rebuild the world-famous Cathedral of Notre Dame de Paris, a year on from the devastating fire that ravaged the 850-year-old Gothic masterpiece. Chief architect Philippe Villeneuve urgently needs a complete picture of the damage sustained during the fire, so he initiates an unprecedented collaboration between architects and scientists. Their mission is to meticulously analyse the fallen timber, stone and fractured glass, to develop a decontamination and restoration plan. This unique opportunity offers a new insight into the medieval materials, techniques and people who built Notre Dame

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores how cathedrals were constructed, looking at fan vaulting at Gloucester, stained glass at York, and the Octagon at Ely for examples of medieval ingenuity, showing how they were built to realise a vision of Heaven on Earth

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a majestic stand of evergreen trees overlooking sweet tiny flowers growing on a sloping knoll

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: The ex-steeplejack tours the coast of north Wales, visiting Harlech, Caernarfon and Beaumaris to admire the defensive features of the strongholds that helped Edward I subdue the Welsh. On the orders of the king, French architect Master James of St Georges revolutionised castle design in England

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a clear sky over a welcoming swimming spot, surrounded by shaded trees

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: On his arrival at Number 10, Jim Hacker is confronted by a confusing nuclear deterrent problem, made more difficult by Sir Humphrey's constant meddling. The new PM soon realises his powers do not stretch to domestic affairs. Political comedy, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Yes, Prime Minister
programme.desc: Jim Hacker prepares to make his first public broadcast as prime minister, but finds it is not so easy to speak to camera as he imagined. John Wells guest stars, alongside Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Thatcher: A Very British Revolution
programme.desc: The rise and dramatic downfall of Margaret Thatcher, as told by members of her inner circle, who reveal how a political outsider won power and came to dominate the 1980s. The first episode charts how she managed to become a prominent political figure and survived the controversial policy that saw the press label her the 'milk snatcher', before challenging Edward Heath for the leadership of the Conservative Party

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: A Liverpool tarmac gang travels to Middlesbrough for a week-long job - only to be tempted by the offer of work on the side. Bernard Hill and Michael Angelis star in Alan Bleasdale's acclaimed 1980 TV play, which inspired the drama serial Boys from the Blackstuff

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: Neel visits Melanie's family to investigate the trafficking of babies, but Rolf soon realises that she could be in danger. Meanwhile, Julita refuse to give up on her stolen child. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.title: DNA
programme.desc: With the people behind the adoption network in custody, Minna is reunited with her mother. Meanwhile, Rolf gets a new lead in the search for his missing daughter. In Danish, English, French and Polish

programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another signature landscape, featuring a clear sky over a welcoming swimming spot, surrounded by shaded trees

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Turning his attentions to timber structures from the Middle Ages the former steeplejack visits England's oldest fortified building with a moat, Stokesay Castle. He also journeys to Harvington Hall near Kidderminster, Worcestershire, to see how bricks became an important part of timber-framed houses

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene depicting an oval winterscape, featuring a cosy barn and lacy bushes

programme.title: New York: America's Busiest City
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with the team who look after all 843 acres of Central Park, revealing the hidden systems and organisational miracles that keep the world's busiest urban park clean and green. Plus, Ant Anstead sees how an entirely new district is being built on top of a functioning rail depot in Manhattan and Dan Snow is in Coney Island, where he discovers that television, air conditioning and extreme weather almost killed off this historic amusement zone

programme.title: True North
programme.desc: Film-maker and wheelchair user Chris Lynch examines attitudes to disability, and the gulf between individuals' quality of life and how they are perceived by able-bodied people. He meets others with various disabilities to learn more about their experiences and general happiness, and uncovers scientific research into happiness and what people can do to maintain it

programme.title: Sex & Drugs & Rock & Roll (2010)
programme.desc: Biopic of highly influential punk singer Ian Dury, examining his lifelong struggle with the disability caused by contracting polio as a child, and the impact his rise to fame in the late 1970s and volatile personality had on those closest to him, particularly his son Baxter. Starring Andy Serkis, Bill Milner, Naomie Harris and Olivia Williams

programme.title: Artificial Things
programme.desc: Stopgap Dance Company bring their celebrated stage work Artificial Things to the screen. Filmed on location in a derelict suburban shopping mall and featuring an ensemble of disabled and non-disabled dancers, this mesmerising film directed by Sophie Fiennes explores human interdependence, strength, and vulnerability

programme.title: John Berger: The Art of Looking
programme.desc: Portrait of the writer and artist to mark his 90th birthday, examining his influential outlook on life and art and his creative collaborations in a variety of mediums, with contributions from theatrical practitioner Simon McBurney, director Michael Dibb, visual artist John Christie, cartoonist Selcuk Demiral, photographer Jean Mohur and two of Bergwer's children - critic Katya and painter Yves. The film is bookended by Berger's experience of cataract removal surgery and rediscovery of the power of seeing

He also shares his passion for helping his community and offers advice for how viewers can have a positive impact on their local areas.

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with potatoes and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels and Duggee buy Hennie a surprise present for her birthday - but first they need to wrap it up

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wufflebump is determined to put on the best puppet show ever, and he wants everything to be perfect.

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid heads to the Wild West, where he tries to learn how to be a proper cowboy by rounding up an escaped bull

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The youngster sees colours coming from the radio. He uses his crayons like a conductor and draws colourful music, but Draff insists that music is for ears, not eyes

programme.title: Tik Tak
programme.desc: Explore a helicopter mosaic and watch as a cheeky chameleon plays with a strawberry and Book Girl plays basketball.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: In the first episode we meet four-year-old Ottalie who lives on a farm. Today Ottalie helps out, collecting fresh eggs from the chickens, cleaning out their run, and giving them new straw. Back inside it's time for a game of hide-and-seek with Mum and little sister Imogen. But when Dad gets home, will he give away Mum's hiding place?

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Woolly reminds Tig how important her Aunt Shonagh's wedding is as the duo do their best to ensure the day is fun for everyone

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Tib suffers some serious misfortune, and it becomes clear it is all down to a sad chap called Bad Luck. Happily, Gigglegoon is on hand to turn their luck around through laughter

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: Timmy Time

programme.title: Melody
programme.desc: Mum and Melody fly a kite, and later the youngster asks for some music about hills or mountains

programme.title: Tee and Mo

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: It is pandemonium at the Alphablocks Animal Sing-song - or does S have a special surprise in store?

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say worm and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We explore everything about worms. There is a long worm, a little worm and a squirmy worm

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Ten turns up to tell the Numberblocks what it's like to be a perfect ten.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: A three-year-old bunny meets friends and experiences emotions as he engages with the world around him

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po collects lots of balloons and takes off into the air, floating high above Teletubbyland, until her balloons pop

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug decide to go holiday and use Granny Pug's guide to camping.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne visits Thomas and William and their kittens as they head off to the vet for their very first check up! Rory travels to a wildlife park to meet the vet team who need to check on some of their new arrivals

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Four-year-old Ottalie is visited by Grandad and Grandma on the farm. They help Ottalie pick apples from the tree, before Ottalie and Grandma get to work making a tasty apple sponge for pudding

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain insists the pirates be more sensible

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog and the squirrels take their teddy bears out for a picnic. However, they soon spot a huge teddy and discover it to be Beryl. Animation for children aged two to five

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Peter invites Jeremy Fisher along for a garden raid, the melodramatic frog gives him more than he bargained for

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all used in airports! Grace uses a baggage loader to unload and load an aeroplane, shows off jet bridges, then uses a pushback tug to move an aeroplane onto the runway!

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When a shell for an orphaned hermit crab gets broken, Andy has to find a replacement

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: There is an amazing seahorse merry-go-round on the beach, and everyone is having lots of fun going round and round - except Bevel. When he runs away from the ride, Bob finds him, only for Bevel to admit he's nervous about going in circles. Everyone joins in to help him overcome his fear and soon he's sitting on his seahorse, Blue Yah, excited about his first turn on a merry-go-round. But now the key that winds it up has broken, so Bitz needs to work out how to get it started using wind power

Luckily, Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan - she and JoJo can make their own film to tide them over, and Jared (a keen filmmaker) can help. JoJo learns all about the camera, acting and editing, eventually producing an amazing film trailer for the gang to watch with popcorn.

programme.desc: Part two of two. The barrister helps Noemie Lopian find out how her French mother survived the war after she was arrested as a child by the Nazis, meeting the grandson of the man who hid her mother and siblings in a chicken shed in his garden during round-ups of Jews in the town of Saint-Junien. Noemie also learns of the incredible sacrifice and courage of a young female French resistance fighter who saved her mother's life

Ricky has selected the Second World War classic set in Japan, Empire of the Sun by JG Ballard

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team visit Wolverhampton, where Henry Nicholls picks up a couple of paintings with very different price tags, while Tony Geering takes on a troublesome twosome. Simon Schneider turns horse dealer and Karen Dalmeny ends up with a very fancy clock

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Omid Djalili in which nine contestants are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they will play for in a final round

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants including Stuart, Sian and Rich pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn makes Jamie an offer, Vinny tells Mandy he is moving out, and Belle's mental health continues to spiral

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter tries to reassure Simon over the Oliver situation, but when Carla has a conflicting view, they become embroiled in a nasty argument, and Carla is left stung. Meanwhile, Leanne tries to persuade Steve to change his mind, and Craig gains an insight into Ray's grand plan

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares a selection of retro dishes inspired by classics of the past, including minced beef, potato and onion pie, served with buttery greens, and finishes off with a queen of puddings. Plus, chef Brian Turner reveals why faggots deserve a comeback

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: After having his taxi stolen, a shaken Peter gives in to his demons. Meanwhile, Ray attempts to buy Craig's silence over the development, and Todd takes a cynical view of Sean's new venture

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: The famous faces get used to life in their new surroundings at Gwrych Castle in Abergele on the north Wales coast as they take part in the trials to try to win food for everyone. Ant and Dec oversee proceedings

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The Millennium Dome Heist with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: The actor reveals the story of the nine-month game of cat-and-mouse between police and a criminal gang intent on stealing diamonds worth £350 million from the Millennium Dome in London in November 2000

programme.title: The Diana Interview: Revenge of a Princess
programme.desc: The conclusion of the two-part documentary examines how the Princess of Wales' 1995 Panorama interview created scandal and division within the BBC itself, leading to a serious rift in the higher echelons of the corporation. The programme examines suggestions that Diana's involvement was gained through the use of fake documents, and features contributions from a graphic designer at the centre of the allegations

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Dating show in which singletons hunt for a potential partner by rifling through three different homes. Yorkshire lass Cheryl takes along her best friend Danielle as she checks out a trio of homes. She has a habit of dating bad boys and is keen to break this trend. Will she find her perfect match in the lads' pad, the swish flat or the rustic farm retreat? Narrated by Rochelle Humes

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Broadway star Josh Groban discusses the US presidential election results and the livestreamed concerts he performed during lockdown, as well as promoting his new album

programme.desc: David Dickinson and the experts help people in Blackpool sort the treasures from their trash, where items include a piece of possessed wood, a knight on horseback, and some Lalique. Henry Nicholls goes on a Tolkien-inspired epic quest, but ends up getting lost

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle is in danger, Will apologises, and Dawn is determined

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking contestants Pete, Suzy and Dan to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000

programme.title: How to Spend It Well at Christmas with Phillip Schofield
programme.desc: The presenter provides festive consumer advice beginning with a guide to toys. He visits a school to see if the classic Rubik's Cube can compete with modern equivalents. Christopher Biggins joins a group of families to test out the latest board games, Angellica Bell and Dallas Campbell test out new educational toys for different age ranges, and Dancing on Ice's Matt Evers road tests inline skates. Plus, consumer journalist Harry Wallop presents a guide to advent calendars

programme.title: I'm a Celebrity Get Me Out of Here!
programme.desc: Gwrych Castle is a far cry from the heat of the Australian jungle, as the contestants discover while adjusting to life on the north Wales coast. As ever, Ant and Dec will be bringing viewers up to date on the latest developments as the trials continue. Will any of the celebrities decide to go home early, or will they stay the course?

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Great Art
programme.desc: Tim Marlow reveals the story behind Monet's greatest paintings, exploring the contrast between his good-humoured love of life and his feelings of loneliness and depression. The programme revisits many of the locations where he painted, and reveals how a passion for gardening led to the glories of his famous Giverny garden

programme.title: Monster Carp
programme.desc: Ali Hamidi, Tom Dove and Neil Spooner visit Thailand, home to the largest carp species in the world - the Siamese carp. These fish feed differently to other varieties, meaning different tactics are required to catch them
