programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: North Londoners Veronica and Jude are looking for a holiday home in Costa de la Luz in southern Spain, so Laura Hamilton shows them five properties within their budget of £110,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole calls in a locksmith to bring a safe back from the scrapheap, and a ship's wheel is left in splinters when the presenter falls out with restorer Guy

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo travel the length of Britain seeking items to fix up, flog on and sell for profit. Max upcycles a brass propeller into a stylish table lamp, while Henry goes all Victorian with an old pond yacht

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: In the Pays de la Loire, twenty-something couple Billy and Gwendoline want to open their chateau as a wedding location, but it's not going to plan as the chimney seems to have caught fire. Near Dijon, Clive and Tanith run an upmarket chateau hotel but need to win over the locals, so they put on an open day. Meanwhile, sisters Tanya and Marian prepare to visit their chateau in the Loire Valley. They need to get it ready for the first guests of the summer in less than a week's time, but are faced with everything from dead flies to leaking swimming pools

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits East Yorkshire, where Nick and Jane are transforming an old bungalow into a contemporary home on a very small budget

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The sports car-themed PitStop in Hertfordshire hosts the first round, as owners Melvyn and Sindy Rutter try to make a good impression. However, the absence of tea and coffee in the rooms does not go down well with the guests, and Melvyn's after-dinner talk about his collection of Morgan cars fails to spark much interest

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gill and Peter Tydie invite their fellow competitors to stay at the Victory in Mersea, Essex, a four-star B&B with 26 members of staff to run the bar, restaurant and accommodation. A trip to a local fishery gives one contestant an opportunity to annoy his peers, and the discovery of a hole in a bedsheet leaves the hosts feeling uneasy about their chances of success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave Botha hosts at his Butterfly Paragliding B&B on the Isle of Wight. Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham are unhappy they must sleep in a tepee in the garden and use an outdoor toilet, and claim they were served watery eggs and rubbery sausages for breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Deborah Morrison and Wayne Bingham host the final night at Braddon Hall in Torquay, Devon, where Melvyn and Sindy take issue with their squeaky four-poster bed. The hoteliers try to entertain their guests by inviting them to take part in an archery contest - which ends in accusations of unfair play

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. One of the hosts gets upset about large underpayments and a couple interrogate their guests about the criticism in their feedback, before the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, keepers prepare for the difficult task of transporting giraffe Kidepo to a new zoo, and Kidepo's brother Narus is not making the move any easier. Meanwhile, silvery gibbon Alvin is keen to get close to his partner Tilu again, and after diving headfirst into an empty pool one too many times, keepers decide a trip to the vet is in order for caiman lizard Gary to see if there is an underlying problem. Elsewhere, dominant warthog Magnum is being challenged by fellow warthog Dobby, so keepers decide it is time to move Dobby to a zoo in Germany

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael locate their heating problem, but it means that Billy - who's scared of height - has to repoint the chimney 20 metres up. Clive and Tanith get their hands dirty trying to work out what's stopped the fountain working at their hotel and Michelin-starred restaurant. Meanwhile, the clock's ticking for sisters Marian and Tanya as they try to sort out a winter's worth of problems

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Building an underground eco bunker that is self-sufficient, sleek and completely one of a kind, and that from the outside will look like a bush garden

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Andy Stakes and Jeanette Hardy, who are building a new house on top of a sheer cliff on the west coast of Scotland. The pair want to spend £250,000 on a two-bedroom, glass-fronted and earth-sheltered home for themselves and their two beloved Newfoundland dogs. However, construction on such an exposed site is fraught with danger and the pair face no end of problems throughout the build, and a last minute design change that begs the biggest question: even if Andy finishes the house, will Jeanette actually like it?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 14-year-old is rushed to St George's after falling head first over the handlebars of her bike and hitting her head, and doctors are concerned she may have caused serious damage. Meanwhile, Geoffrey, who is 56, has been urgently transferred from another hospital after a severe infection left him with a compromised airway and struggling to breathe

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With police numbers lower than they've been for decades, this edition focuses on the increased demands on front-line police in Northamptonshire. Call handlers are finding their work increasingly demoralising, while the squeeze on resources is not helped by the public's ever-broadening sense of what constitutes an emergency

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words and numbers quiz. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 14-year-old is rushed to St George's after falling head first over the handlebars of her bike and hitting her head, and doctors are concerned she may have caused serious damage. Meanwhile, Geoffrey, who is 56, has been urgently transferred from another hospital after a severe infection left him with a compromised airway and struggling to breathe

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: With police numbers lower than they've been for decades, this edition focuses on the increased demands on front-line police in Northamptonshire. Call handlers are finding their work increasingly demoralising, while the squeeze on resources is not helped by the public's ever-broadening sense of what constitutes an emergency

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to Babylon in New York, where the peace of Long Island is regularly shattered by the volatile Pellegrino family, proprietors of Peter's Italian Restaurant. Gordon finds owner Tina's work to save the eatery from oblivion is frequently ruined by the behaviour of her brother, a tyrannical front-of-house manager whose violent temper constantly undermines staff morale

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton and Matt Tebbutt revisit more of their favourite investigations, as Jimmy travels deep underground in Sicily to a subterranean salt processing plant and Kate learns how coconut oil is extracted. Plus, Matt looks into how manufacturers get the flavours onto crisps

programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp introduces new crafts, skills and techniques for interior decoration. In this edition, she reveals how to create a personalised vintage doormat and coasters, using spray paint, old tiles and serviettes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Derbyshire couple Kim and Rob feel the time is right to buy a holiday home in Andalusia. They love the traditional white villages in the region - the Pueblos Blancos - and are looking for a bolthole to enjoy sunny breaks in with their four children and three grandchildren. Danni Menzies utilises all her skills to find something perfect within their exacting budget of £50,000

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain looking for items to fix up and flog on behalf of their owners. In this episode they're close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles from horse owner Sabrina and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a handsome profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael locate their heating problem, but it means that Billy - who's scared of height - has to repoint the chimney 20 metres up. Clive and Tanith get their hands dirty trying to work out what's stopped the fountain working at their hotel and Michelin-starred restaurant. Meanwhile, the clock's ticking for sisters Marian and Tanya as they try to sort out a winter's worth of problems

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton visits rural Northamptonshire, where new couple Rob and Jan want to build a vicarage-style home to carry them into retirement. Rob and Jan aren't big on decision-making, and it remains to be seen whether Charlie will succeed on getting them to agree on anything

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and Shirley Boyle, owners of the Old Hall Country House in Madeley, Cheshire, host the week's first round of the B&B competition. The guests are impressed by the 16th-century building, and are taken tap-dancing and to a private fine-dining restaurant, but one of them is dissatisfied with her bedroom's layout and the garnish on her eggs at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. The hostess's pet cats are not to all of her guests' liking, and with activities including a tour of a feline cemetery, the theme for the stay is made abundantly clear

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are the third hosts, at their three-bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, Cornwall. For the activity they arrange a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. Some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room but the food is a success, and Eileen Finn demonstrates her karate skills on the men

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. On arrival, the competitors are impressed by the 'pink palace', but things go downhill when a bed is broken and one couple are allocated a room next to the front door. However, Eileen is delighted by the feline memorabilia in her room and the group enjoys a spot of indoor paint-balling and an open-top bus tour of the resort

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and to air their grievances. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is surprised by an overpayment and Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley receives tough criticism, before the winner is announced

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Thirty seven-year-old elephant Thi is pregnant for the ninth time but the keepers believe she is now three months' overdue. The risks for Thi and her unborn calf are growing by the day, and the staff are worried. Rampant male Rothschild giraffe Meru causes problems for the giraffe herd as he relentlessly chases the females, and male clownfish Polo gets injured while fighting with the other males for the attentions of dominant female Rosie. However, clownfish can change sex at will, and Polo's time alone in an isolation tank produces a surprising side effect

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy, Gwendoline and Michael finally hope to move into their chateau so they can host a hen party, although the kitchen's unfinished. In the Loire Valley, Paul and Karen rent out their chateau as a holiday gite hoping to attract more guests by creating an office in an old water tower. Meanwhile, Dick and Angel try to keep costs down when shopping for baths

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets Mike and Megan, who are building a modernist house on a modest footprint in Suffolk Park, New South Wales

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets entrepreneur Paul Wilkinson and his wife Amy, who are building a giant new home on a 16-acre plot in Lincolnshire. The superstructure will be comprised of five circular buildings made up of interlocking timber cylinders, linked by raised wooden walkways that hang over a fishing lake. The complicated build proves difficult for Paul's local team, and their ambitious schedule of completing the project in a year soon looks out of reach

programme.title: The Kennedys: Tragedy & Triumph
programme.desc: Series charting the lives of Joseph and Rose Kennedy and their children, uncovering how and why this family so significantly impacted 20th century America. The final episode takes viewers behind the public image of America's 'First Family', revealing how personal relationships within the Kennedy dynasty helped shape national and global events

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Long-distance lorry driver Neil, 52, has fallen backwards off a ramp onto a concrete floor and is sent for an urgent CT scan, the results of which are a shock. Also receiving treatments are 29-year-old Ben, who has dislocated his shoulder in a cycling accident, and Wendy, 73, who is in resus with a severe open ankle fracture after falling at home

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words and numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Danny Dyer taking on Joe Wilkinson and Gabby Logan, while David O'Doherty joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Nightwatch Nation: Emergency 911
programme.desc: In Baton Rouge, Lacey and Stacey restrain a patient who is suffering from violent hallucinations after overdosing on a synthetic drug. Matt and Henry face a difficult challenge

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Long-distance lorry driver Neil, 52, has fallen backwards off a ramp onto a concrete floor and is sent for an urgent CT scan, the results of which are a shock. Also receiving treatments are 29-year-old Ben, who has dislocated his shoulder in a cycling accident, and Wendy, 73, who is in resus with a severe open ankle fracture after falling at home

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Labrador is rushed to Fitzpatrick's unable to stand, and although Professor Noel Fitzpatrick tells the family that surgery is not an option, his team devises an alternative care plan. The vets also treat a bichon frise suffering chronic elbow pain, and a leonberger has a bone tumour removed from its leg after being diagnosed with cancer

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team revisits favourite investigations, including Kate Quilton's visit to the world's largest producer of jelly beans to find out what goes into the sweets. Matt Tebbutt attempts to find out why tinned anchovies taste so salty, and Jimmy Doherty heads to a Mauritius distillery as he explores the secrets of rum

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Danielle spends too much on the remains of a Native American chief, but Frank hatches an ambitious plan to cover their losses

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: The bargain hunters head to an airport auction and compete for luggage and plane parts

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Former boxer Brandon Bernier and his wife Lori are anxious to see what is inside a locked coffin, but a rival thwarts their plans

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank meet a woman who has a vast collection of valuables but does not like to sell things. They also race to find treasure in a barn before it falls down

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike and Frank meet a man with a collection of classic cars, movie memorabilia and aviator helmets

programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: The work taking place in one of Britain's leading police car workshops, as bespoke vehicles are built and repairs made to ones that have been out in the field. In the first episode, John Heussi finds himself in need of a new riot van, and stress-testing the old one helps him design a new one

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff heads to Iceland to compete in the world's maddest motorsport, while Paddy McGuinness gets to grips with the latest Toyota Supra. Chris Harris investigates the story of the Lotus 79, arguably the most beautiful F1 car of all time

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuiness, Chris Harris and Freddie Flintoff go on a road trip in a trio of affordable second-hand convertibles. On the test track, Chris puts the new Ariel Atom through its paces, while Freddie goes bungee jumping off a dam in an old Rover

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef's gastronomic road trip from Venice to Istanbul through the countries of the former Byzantine Empire is nearing its end as he leaves southern Greece for the shores of western Turkey. On the Cesme Peninsula Rick enjoys creamy Armola cheese matured in goat skins, Urla's famous red wine and the ultimate Turkish breakfast. He also cooks the country's iconic dish sultan's delight - a mouth-watering lamb stew on a bed of aubergine puree

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan joins the team in the studio. Chris Harris races the £2.5million McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's latest fighter jet, and Paddy McGuinness tries out a cheap but luxurious off-roader

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris are sent on a high-altitude mountain expedition across Peru in second-hand American cars. Plus, back at the track, Chris tests out the latest electric car from Porsche

programme.title: Eddie Eats America
programme.desc: Eddie visits the Garden State for supersized breakfast in a New Jersey diner, a tryout with ice hockey pros the NJ Devils - and an epic struggle with an eight-hot-dog challenge

programme.title: Travel Man: 48 Hours In
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and comedy actor Jack Dee embark on a clock-conscious exploration of the Italian city, enjoying two days rich in humour, history, culture and pizza. The pair start with a guided tour on vintage Vespas amid the city's chaotic traffic, attend a pizza-making class and then head to the volcanic ruins of Herculaneum. Day two sees them visit the Isle of Capri and play the lottery using la smorfia, a local guide to the meaning of dreams

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Quiz in which four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with BBC Breakfast's Naga Munchetty, singer and actor Jordan Stephens, presenter and actress Sally Lindsay and comedian David O'Doherty kicking thing off. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents a special edition looking back over the best moments from the series. programme.desc: The chef embarks on a culinary journey by sea and land to explore the Far East's most diverse food cultures. He begins in Cambodia, where tourists are beginning to return after the fall of the Khmer Rouge regime. As well as discovering the joys of freshly caught swimmer crabs with green peppercorns, he learns how to cook the country's most famous dish - fish amok - and samples lok lak, made with marinated beef. He also visits the Angkor Wat temple and joins a rice barge to travel down the Mekong River

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: Hitler's naval commander Karl Donitz, a fanatical Nazi who would do anything to support the party's aims. After the war, he claimed he'd only been a sailor following orders

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: As Hitler's personal lawyer, Hans Frank twisted the law to help the Führer's agenda, and became known as the Butcher of Poland. James finds out how he became a power-hungry monster

programme.title: Medieval Dead
programme.desc: Tim Sutherland, Malin Holst and Simon Richardson investigate the discovery of barrels containing well-preserved swords on the site of the final battle of the Hundred Years War

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Acclaimed chronicle of the Second World War, beginning with an account of Hitler's ascent to power at a time when the Germans were stricken by humiliating military defeat, runaway inflation and economic depression. The Nazi leader's promises of regained national pride and prosperity struck a chord with millions of ordinary citizens, and he was elected on a tidal wave of popular support. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: The largest evacuation of the Second World War, which saw two million civilians and soldiers removed in an extraordinary German operation that lasted for four months

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Ruins from the time before the fall of the Berlin Wall, including a covert Communist base that hid Cold War submarines for Tito and the former HQ of the Soviet Army in East Germany

programme.title: Hitler's Olympics: The Boys of '36
programme.desc: The story of the 1936 Olympics held in Berlin, revealing how the games led to Nazi Germany gaining international praise as a nation that had triumphed following the Depression

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Hanbury Hall in Droitwich. Among the rarities brought in to be valued are a jacket designed by Peter Blake, which proves less valuable for being nibbled by a ferret, a nude painting and a sketch of Napoleon on his deathbed. Andy McConnell examines the oldest piece of glass he has seen in his career, and Fiona is challenged to spot a 2500-year-old Greek vessel among three fakes

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of the train operating company's drivers, managers, ticket inspectors and technicians along the route between London, south-west England and south Wales. In the first edition, Paddington's duty station manager Simon Jeffery has his customer service skills tested by several angry passengers who have been held up following a fire, while train manager Jodie deals with drunks on a late-night service from Swansea

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A multi-billion-pound 32km super sewer under the streets of London, an early sewer system at the Palace of Knossos and a pivotal piece of tunnel boring equipment from the past

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A closer look at a sprawling Wild West ghost town that sits peaceful and abandoned in Montana, USA, but is still haunted by tales of murder, corruption and buried treasure

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: A Scottish soldier who walked 200 miles through the Libyan desert without water or supplies recalls Ronald Moore, the leader of his party

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The months following prime minister Neville Chamberlain's declaration of war against Germany, in which the only casualties on the home front were those who had to endure the blackouts. Elsewhere in Europe, the Nazi war machine cut a swathe of destruction. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Bohemia and Bavaria, beginning by exploring the art nouveau architecture of Czech Republic capital Prague. He then samples the sulphurous spa waters and wallows in peat and mud in Marianske Lazne, formerly known as Marienbad, before test driving a state-of-the-art passenger train made at the Skoda factory in Pilsen. Crossing the border into Germany, Michael encounters a fire-breathing dragon in Furth-im-Wald, and learns about an early 20th-century pioneer who laid the foundations for Munich's pre-eminence in science and technology

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Music festival goers descend on Glasgow, a street art mystery is revealed, and the oldest and youngest train cleaners on the station set to work

programme.title: Steam Train Britain
programme.desc: Ffestiniog and Welsh Highland Railway are celebrating their annual Victorian Weekend, while in south Devon, silver service waiter Andy Leech runs the three-course Sunday lunch

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn visits the architecture of the Stockton & Darlington railway, the first passenger steam line in the world and learns how Essex's enormous Chappel Viaduct was built

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a second visit to Trentham Gardens near Stoke-on-Trent, where items featured include an early camera which had previously been used for natural history photography, a dagger with a gruesome history, a shawl that once belonged to a movie star, and a small 18th-century figurine

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Staff cope with the pressure of rush hour during the summer, with Lorel Kinghorn trying to get a group of rowdy schoolchildren safely on board a train without causing major delays on the line. There is a chance to find out what happens during the routine weekly maintenance, and Network Rail's Steve Hawkins and Emma Taylor take to the air as part of their commitment to preserve some of the most historic parts of the railway

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Whistler on the Canadian Pacific seaboard, a remote mountain town whose creation was made possible with record-breaking engineering equipment, helping establish one of the best ski resorts in the world

programme.desc: A ghostly house in a tropical paradise that is still feared by the locals and a pioneering complex marked by both glory and tragedy that will soon to be lost forever

programme.title: Narrow Escapes of World War Two
programme.desc: An examination of how Leon Degrelle and an array of Finns, Estonians and fellow Belgians joined German volunteer brigades to hold off the Red Army on the Eastern front during the Second World War and enabled 56,000 men to escape

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The drop in morale of Churchill's forces following the fall of France to Nazi forces during the Second World War, and how the Dunkirk evacuation and the Battle of Britain helped to restore British confidence. Plus, America's reasons for initially refusing to enter the conflict

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures once more on to the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaw's 1913 publication Continental Guide, beginning by travelling through Russia. He starts in the industrial city of Tula, before visiting Leo Tolstoy's former home of Yasnaya Polyana and learning how the author's life and works were intertwined with the railways. Michael then boards the train that runs from the Caspian Sea to Moscow, where he performs an important role in a dramatic opera at the Bolshoi Theatre, before exploring the beauty and history of St Petersburg and riding on the first railway ever built in Russia

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores London's Restoration history, including the founding of the Royal Society and the violent dark side behind Charles II's carefully crafted public image. She visits St Paul's Cathedral, reveals how women performed on stage for the first time during this period, and uncovers shocking details of the slave trade at the National Maritime Museum. Plus, a look at parallels between the plague that struck London during the 1660s and the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree's to-do list is as long as ever, and with no guests, weddings or events at the chateau this year, they use their engineering and design skills to tackle some major projects. First on the list is transforming one of the old outbuildings into a supersized workshop for chateau-sized jobs

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on programmes including Strictly Come Dancing, I'm a Celebrity... A Jungle Story, 24 Hours in Police Custody, Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat and Naked Attraction

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present this year's semi-final, the last four bakers facing three patisserie challenges to decide who will be going through to next week's final. The signature round features plenty of alcohol, while the technical challenge sets the bakers the task of making pastry horns. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must use all the precision they can muster to construct a cake made entirely from smaller cakes

programme.title: Educating Greater Manchester
programme.desc: The pressure is on at Harrop Fold as GCSE exams get underway, and the school finds itself hitting the headlines when four members of staff are suspended over allegations of removing disadvantaged and struggling pupils from the school roll to improve exam results. The new heads girl launches an online campaign against uniform policy that lands her in trouble. Mr Walker is concerned that year 10 pupil Morgan may not be able to catch up with Spanish in time for her exams, and Mr Povey intervenes to find out the underlying cause of her disruptive behaviour

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more people looking for love to takje their pick from six nude candidates. Retired dentist Leanne is recently divorced and in search of a sexually experimental man, while former US marine Conor, who falls in love all too easily, is looking for his dream Englishwoman

programme.title: Dance Around the World
programme.desc: Three of Europe's most talented dancers take off on a global quest to find the beating heart and cultural roots of street dance. Throughout the series, the trio step out of their comfort zones, searching for dancing communities who have inspired the art form and discovering the roots of local dance culture. In the first episode, Suzette, Kym and Maren travel to Brazil, where they learn about the fast-paced fusion-dance passinho, before travelling to a rural settlement in the jungle outside Rio, where they are shown the art of capoeira, an ancient dance-fight born from the time of slavery

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Sheila Hancock and broadcaster Gyles Brandreth tackle their first manual lock and some terrifyingly tight turns, but find themselves relaxing into life at 4mph, and marvel at how secret histories reveal themselves. They visit the Royal Gunpowder Mills, which built its own canal system to transport volatile ammunition - vital to Britain's success in both world wars

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mother-of-three Parveen Ashraf from Peterborough aims to dazzle her guests with an Asian-themed menu consisting of nine dishes, as well as entertainment provided by former X Factor contestant Nicole Lawrence. However, insurance worker Shane Jackson's incessant talking irritates the other guests and threatens to ruin the night

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Insurance broker Shane Jackson throws the second party in Peterborough, but has a daunting task ahead of him after offending previous host Parveen Ashraf by refusing to eat her food. A series of culinary mishaps in the kitchen fails to put a damper on the evening, and while Carolle Barlow makes a shocking announcement, Aly Nicholson reveals a surprise under her dress

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third day of the competition in Peterborough sees catering manager Carolle Barlow trying to impress her guests with a 1970s-themed evening, but does not take into account the generation gap with the other contestants. The host's two pet chihuahuas also make an unwelcome appearance at the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Healthcare assistant Aly Nicholson hosts the penultimate dinner party in Peterborough and decides to serve two courses she has never cooked before. She is determined to create a harmonious evening, but makes the mistake of starting a conversation about first impressions

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pole-dance instructor Ricky Dent hopes to impress during the final party in Peterborough, but his evening does not go to plan when his spicy starter burns Shane Jackson's tongue. After distracting the group with a personal revelation, he tries to wow the amateur chefs by demonstrating his dance moves

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lynne and her sister Debbie seek the perfect place on the beautiful Costa Teguise in Lanzarote. After Lynne's husband Frankie was diagnosed with a terminal illness, Lynne took early retirement to care for him. A holiday home in the sun has been a lifelong dream for this couple, and with Frankie's illness they don't want to wait any longer. With a budget of £150,000, Ben Hillman lines up five suitable properties for them to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Kerry Herbert from Brighton has dreamed of buying a property on Grenada since she visited the Caribbean island as a child. With her teenage son about to fly the nest and after dealing with the loss of her father, she has decided to take the plunge and buy somewhere that she, her son and her partner can enjoy. Jonnie Irwin helps shows her five properties - but it remains to be seen whether any of them will tempt Kerry to make an offer

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Douglas Hotel in Hartlepool, where owner Raqeeb Ramzan and his uncle Raj Malik hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Nigel tackles dated windows and Debbie creates her first mural as the duo set to work transforming a room in a rundown conservatory into a bright new garden room

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray thinks something is wrong and confides in Debra when he has a miserable night with his friends, but an invitation to join Frank for a steam bath restores his spirits. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Peter Boyle

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra give Robert $1,000 to help him out of dire financial straits - but he takes advantage of their generosity by jetting off for a holiday in Las Vegas

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a photographer to find a new home on the Greek island of Skopelos in time for his 60th birthday, showcasing five properties on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Maggie and Alistair from Edinburgh dream of buying a holiday home on the southern coast of Gran Canaria. Jasmine Harman lines up five potential properties for their £350,000 budget

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding present this year's semi-final, the last four bakers facing three patisserie challenges to decide who will be going through to next week's final. Finally, for the Showstopper, they must use all the precision they can muster to construct a cake made entirely from smaller cakes

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The competing comics demonstrate their love of watermelon and Johnny tries to win points by grabbing a passing cyclist and introducing him to some Christmas-related equipment. Katherine Parkinson, Daisy May Cooper, Mawaan Rizwan, Richard Herring and Johnny Vegas take part in another round of ludicrous challenges judged by Greg Davies, with Alex Horne keeping score

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more people looking for love to takje their pick from six nude candidates. Retired dentist Leanne is recently divorced and in search of a sexually experimental man, while former US marine Conor, who falls in love all too easily, is looking for his dream Englishwoman

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Featuring the stories of three patients filmed at St George's in south London, including a retired air stewardess who was rushed into hospital after seriously cutting her leg as her best friend's 70th birthday party. Monica arrives in A&E with hip pain, and talks about growing up in Trinidad and her early years working in the UK as a nurse, and patient Christine has stroke-like symptoms and is suffering with tremors on the left side of her body

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back at previous first dates that did not go smoothly, including make-up artist Demi's awkward meeting with 29-year-old Danny, and retired NHS counsellor Elaine's date with music producer Steve, which appeared to be going well until it came to settling the bill. Plus, a look back at Disney superfans James and Jess's date

programme.title: Heston's Feasts
programme.desc: Reinventing dishes that remind Heston Blumenthal of the 1960s, a decade he believes is best represented by the psychedelia of Roald Dahl's book Charlie and the Chocolate Factory. His themed menu for actress Tamsin Egerton, entertainer Patti Boulaye, singer Mica Paris, TV presenters Tim Lovejoy and Ben Shephard and broadcaster Mike Read features lickable wallpaper, duck a l'orange and a chocolate waterfall with edible rocks

programme.title: Crazy Delicious
programme.desc: Jayde Adams presents the culinary contest, with astrophysics enthusiast Harry, flavour maverick Elainea and Italian dessert specialist Gabi taking up the challenge to come up with new twists on classic dishes. The first two challenges see them pushed to come up with a unique recipe involving apples and their own take on spaghetti bolognese. After the first two challenges, one contestant will be eliminated and the remaining two must prepare brunch for judges Heston Blumenthal, Niklas Ekstedt and Carla Hall

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George's continued renovation mission takes him to Moseley and Wheaton Aston in the West Midlands. He begins with a conversion to unite upstairs and downstairs flats in an Arts and Crafts building, although one of his suggestions leaves the owners speechless. He also visits an 18th-century pub that has fallen victim to some dodgy DIY - and quickly devises a clever plan to make the most of the period features

programme.desc: Will's ex-boyfriend Michael returns, giving the impression that he wants to pick up where they left off. Karen takes stock of her position at Grace Adler Designs and decides she needs more responsibility

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: The gang spends a weekend at a rural cabin in an effort to take Will's mind off his ex-boyfriend. While Karen's culinary efforts go up in smoke, Grace rekindles an old romance. Eric McCormack and Debra Messing star

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie meets Berta's grand-daughter Prudence, who quickly takes an unwanted interest in him. He asks Rose to pretend she is still his girlfriend as a cover - but the lovelorn old flame takes her role a little too seriously. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: A long-time crush of Charlie finally agrees to go on a date with him, as long as she can bring her sister - who will also need an escort. Alan is persuaded to step in, even though he has caught flu from Jake, but the entire evening is soon jeopardised. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In Bristol, eviction lawyer Chris deals with a complex case in which squatters have broken into an empty pub and are now refusing to leave, with landlord Alex wanting them out as he is awaiting planning permission to turn the pub into flats. Jill is trying to regain a property she and her late husband bought as an investment, which she has been renting out to alleviate financial stress

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Harrow Council carries out early morning raids on several dangerous properties, only to find vulnerable tenants in uninhabitable conditions, including one man living in a plywood shed on a roof. Plus, two more cases of nightmare tenants. Nisha lost her job just as her tenants stopped paying the rent, while Andy agreed to rent his north London home to a friend - who ended up subletting the house and pocketing the profits for himself

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, agents Matt and Garry chase £1,700 owed by an accountant to a client, and officers Max and Paul try to recover almost £30,000 in north west London owed in rent arrears by a dentist after he was barred from working. Meanwhile, agents Max and Steve head to south west London to chase almost £2,000 owed by a cafe owner

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester to recover one of the largest debts they have ever chased - more than £400,000 - owed to a builder after he was failed to receive payment for his work on a house. Meanwhile, officers Gary and Cona are in Kent to chase over £11,000 owed by a couple for unpaid rent

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Max and Steve head to London to recover more than £21,000 in unpaid court costs after a car accident. Officers Aron and Cona team up to recover almost £7,000 owed after a debtor failed to repay a loan to her ex-partners

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An evasive man in west London is given a choice between paying up or relinquishing his luxury car, there's a writ for the organiser of a music festival, an aggressive rent defaulter needs evicting, and an entire hole family is angered in Hertfordshire when the agents call to collect a loan owed to a woman's ex-partner

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers are kept busy with public order patrols as World Cup fever hits Essex, and an ambitious thief tries to steal a 200-gallon fuel tank. In a hunt for stolen vehicles, the Air Support Unit is forced to shut the Dartford Crossing, and a suspected drug dealer cannot give a reasonable explanation for a suspicious nosebleed

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police helicopter pursues an armed robber who is trying to evade arrest, and the team's new recruits put their detective skills to the test by apprehending suspected burglars. Elsewhere, PC Russ Gay tries to calm down a group of unruly drunken youths and Zippy the sniffer dog makes an interesting discovery inside a stolen car

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: After Scarlett's attack backfires, she is gravely injured, and Ned makes her confess to her disturbing plans. Paul stumbles upon Pierce and Dipi in a heated office-based moment

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Tori nearly interrogates Christian on their date, Justin looks into radiotherapy, and Ziggy forces Tane to move the stolen goods out of the Garage

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: One inventive dad thinks he has a solution to reduce the amount of accidents outside of schools, but will his pop-up zebra crossing make the streets around his children's school a safer place? Elsewhere in the programme, cameras join the bus drivers of Bath, whose routes are peppered with evidence of careless parking

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Cameras focus on Sheffield-born market trader Jacqui, who needs to make as much as she can at the tourist night markets on the Costa del Sol prom. There's also a look at cockney former publican Victor, who has spent the past 18 years living in his motor home. But with only his pension to live on, and his movable mansion in need of a refurb, will he have to sell up?

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams aiming to clean up the country, including internet entrepreneur Martin, and his partner-in-grime Glen, who have had enough of the fly-tippers plaguing their beloved Hampshire countryside and have decided to fight back using all means at their disposal. In the North East, the Howdon Sewage Treatment plant not only cleans over 12,000 litres of waste water a second, but even manages to turn it into a gas used to heat homes across the region. But to do it, head honcho Tony has to make sure his plant remains blockage free under a constant assault from wet wipes, sanitary products, false teeth and even the occasional 20ft python

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War with the Law
programme.desc: At West Orchards in Coventry, senior officer Lewis turns detective when a stallholder reports a theft an hour after it happened. He reviews the CCTV footage, pinpoints the incident and tracks down the perpetrator outside the shopping centre. A department store calls in a suspected perfume theft, but until they can review their footage and confirm it security officer Mal keeps track of the suspect on CCTV

programme.title: 5 Clues That Caught the Killer: Rhys Jones
programme.desc: Documentary examining the investigation into the murder of 11-year-old Rhys Jones, a schoolboy shot dead on his way home from football training in 2007. The programme looks at how police managed to make the vital breakthroughs that finally put Rhys' killer behind bars, featuring interviews with police officers and specialists directly involved in the case

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers in South Yorkshire's Road Crime Unit, which is based in Sheffield, pursue a drunken driver and join forces with the helicopter to track down a wanted criminal on a motorcycle. Meanwhile, a new driver crashes into an unmarked police car

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Pierce needs to choose between selling his shares, or admit to his affair. Jane tries to understand why Nicolette is so driven to find closure with Mrs Mangel

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dean gets frustrated with Amber over her refusal to let Jai meet Karen, and Angelo sets a trap to force Colby and Taylor into an incriminating situation

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Following the hidden army of enforcement teams aiming to clean up the country, including internet entrepreneur Martin, and his partner-in-grime Glen, who have had enough of the fly-tippers plaguing their beloved Hampshire countryside and have decided to fight back using all means at their disposal. In the North East, the Howdon Sewage Treatment plant not only cleans over 12,000 litres of waste water a second, but even manages to turn it into a gas used to heat homes across the region. But to do it, head honcho Tony has to make sure his plant remains blockage free under a constant assault from wet wipes, sanitary products, false teeth and even the occasional 20ft python

programme.title: Aldi vs Lidl: Supermarket Wars
programme.desc: The success of the two German-owned supermarket chains, investigating their canny marketing ploys and aggressive sales techniques in their battle for domination. Insiders reveal the secrets behind both supermarkets' success and how they compete with their rivals, while the programme highlights the most tempting cut-price products that are the key to their success

programme.title: Harlan Coben's Safe
programme.desc: Tom is in for a shock when he learns that Sophie has not been honest with him, and is carrying a deadly, final secret. Thriller, starring Michael C Hall

programme.title: 25 Myths About Lesbians
programme.desc: Documentary charting the lesbian movement, exploring myths born from an archaic past, including an assessment of Queen Victoria's views, and a look at global gay figureheads

programme.title: Plus-Size Porn: Adults Only
programme.desc: The world of supersized sex workers, women at different stages of embracing their size and moving from being labelled unattractive to billing themselves as big and beautiful. The programme follows Lisa, aka Lady Voluptua and porn stars Saskia and Vicky as they ply their trades and stake their claims for bigger women to be considered serious players in the sex industry

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: The specialist clinic opens its doors to patients with acne, psoriasis and cysts.

programme.title: Wentworth Prison
programme.desc: Modern-day reimagining of 1970s and 80s prison-set drama Prisoner: Cell Block H

programme.title: Wentworth Prison
programme.desc: A new inmate, Sheila Bausch, arrives at Wentworth charged with multiple counts of murder, and she is part of the cult Lou and Reb escaped from, which opens old wounds

programme.title: Empire
programme.desc: Cookie and Lucious prepare Treasure for her big break, and a tech entrepreneur takes over as CEO of Empire Records, with some surprising new ideas for the company

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: When a seductive conman is killed, Castle and Beckett must investigate New York's elite. Meanwhile, Castle plans to surprise Beckett on their one-year wedding anniversary

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: The author and Beckett investigate a murder on a cruise ship and discover the vessel was being used to smuggle uncut heroin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Assistant DAs McCoy and Kincaid put their careers on the line in search of the truth behind the death of a wealthy woman. Meanwhile, detectives question the victim's first and second husbands, and her teenage daughter

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A routine investigation brings to light evidence that prompts Briscoe and Logan to pick up the trail of a political fugitive who went underground following a 1971 burglary that left a police officer dead. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lt Van Buren is mugged by two teenagers, but the hold-up does not go according to plan and the officer discharges her weapon, killing one of the youngsters with a shot to the back. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the abduction and murder of a teacher, and later discover it's possible that she knew the culprit. Having arrested one of her students, the courts quickly decide their evidence is inadmissible - but it seems another assailant could be to blame. Benjamin Bratt and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's body is found in a lift, and McCoy finds his prosecution of the prime suspect hampered by a vindictive judge Ross offended in court

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A student is found stabbed to death in his flat, and a college friend is soon named prime suspect - but she claims the victim was a stranger who used a date rape drug on her. However, it soon emerges that the crime could have been racially motivated. Drama, with Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman is drugged and raped while working behind the bar at a party for a billionaire businessman

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A woman makes a false accusation, leaving her son faced with a deadly choice. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team sets out to track down a serial rapist, and traces the culprit to a homeless shelter, where the trail goes cold. Undeterred, Carisi decides to go undercover in an attempt to reveal the perpetrator's identity. New York-set police crime drama, directed by and starring Mariska Hargitay, with Peter Scanavino

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team tries to prevent a miscarriage of justice after a young model is raped by a renowned fashion photographer, who looks to his successful brother to make the charges disappear. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Detectives Logan and Wheeler investigate when a popular online video blogger is apparently kidnapped during a live broadcast, but begin to doubt the crime really occurred. However, the case takes a strange turn when a ransom demand is made. Michelle Trachtenberg guest stars, and veteran US news reporter Larry King appears as himself

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A pregnant Muslim student is murdered, and initial inquiries suggest the crime was prompted by her efforts to break up a clash between two ethnic groups. However, Logan and Wheeler later learn the victim was secretly dating an Italian-American against the wishes of her parents, who had organised an arranged marriage - and as they investigate the animosity between the two families, more blood is shed

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: The author takes the stand as the key witness in a murder trial, but then new info emerges late in the case, leading him and Beckett to rush to prevent a miscarriage of justice. Detective drama, starring Nathan Fillion and Stana Katic

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A logbook is Logan's only lead when Arthur Kopinsky is found dead at his law firm. A closer look at the evidence suggests suspects in the form of a swindler who stole a woman's fortune, and the son of his target, who was once wealthy himself. Starring Chris Noth and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The body of a drug addict is discovered in the yard of a daycare centre, and evidence reveals she was the heiress to the fortune. However, suspicions are raised when the family's lawyer gets power of attorney over the trust fund. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An abortion clinic doctor is murdered, and when Logan and Briscoe investigate, they begin to suspect members of a radical movement led by a former priest, who appears to have no sympathy for the man's fate. Starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the murder of a rare coin dealer, focusing their efforts on a millionaire who seems the most likely culprit - but Ross finds himself forced to play detective too. Sam Waterson and Jerry Orbach star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Curtis uncovers evidence of police corruption that puts the DA's office into conflict with an ambitious judge, and Briscoe comes under investigation for stealing evidence from a police lockup. Crime drama, with Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A janitor is found murdered, leading the team to a schizophrenic student they discover has been part of an experimental drug programme. Though all evidence points to the youngster, it transpires that he had no motive to commit the crime and the police soon turn their attentions to a psychiatrist. Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is found murdered in Quantico, mere hours after calling Gibbs to discuss a possible case. In response, Bishop and McGee go undercover as a married couple to run surveillance on a lieutenant and his wife, who may be connected to the death. Starring Mark Harmon, Emily Wickersham and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A young Navy Seaman is found dead, with his eyes gouged out, after being involved in a car accident. Director Vance makes a rare return to field duty, pairing up with Gibbs to investigate when it becomes clear the killing seems to be connected to the case of Kip Klugman, a former NCIS agent he once locked up for stealing evidence. Starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Eames investigate the contract killing of a businessman's wife. Evidence at the scene points to two assailants, one of whom was wearing a bandage, and the victim's mother-in-law becomes the chief suspect. Starring Vincent D'Onofrio, Kathryn Erbe and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates a youth hockey team, where bullying of new players has taken a dangerous turn

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Fin revisits his military roots when a fellow Army ranger struggles to recover from a sexual assault. Sarah Booth guest stars, with Ice T and Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A contestant on a reality dating show claims to have been raped. Co-operating with the SVU, the programme's producers agree to let Benson and her team go undercover on the set for an incognito investigation. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A corrections officer is arrested, accused of sexually assaulting his female charges both inside the prison system and beyond. As the case against the man begins to take shape, the union that supports him takes aim at Barba and the other members of the unit. Crime drama, starring Raul Esparza

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A woman and her date are murdered in her apartment while enjoying a romantic evening in. Though Goren and Eames initially suspect the married father of her 18-month-old daughter, they soon begin to believe that members of her rich family were involved in the crime - and may have killed before. Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe star, with Doris Roberts and Richard Kind

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A candidate for the next New York mayoral elections finds her campaign in jeopardy when Goren and Eames investigate her husband on suspicion of hiring a hitman to kill a judge. Xander Berkeley guest stars, with Vincent D'Onofrio and Kathryn Erbe

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The shooting of a board-member at an exclusive private school, and a man's ambitions for his offspring are stretched to the limit when he admits to the crime, despite the fact his son was the gunman. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a crooked car dealer whose son is dying of leukaemia presents McCoy and Ross with a moral dilemma. His ex-wife is their prime suspect - but it transpires that the victim had regularly abused her and refused to pay child support. Drama, starring Sam Waterston

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of Richard Speigel, chief financial officer for an exclusive, family owned department store, results in the arrests of the shop staff - but the manager and father refuses to let his offspring take the blame, and goes all out to get them off the hook. Drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The case against a man accused of shooting the leader of the African-American Congress is jeopardised by the revelation that the prosecution's chief witness was once an informant for the FBI, and is still under their protection

Meanwhile, Bishop returns home to Oklahoma for Thanksgiving. Military crime drama, starring Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS

Crime drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The notorious Tri-State Sniper appears to have claimed another victim when a petty officer is shot dead in a crowded park. However, when the team discovers the killer in question was already in custody on the day of the shooting, it becomes clear a copy-cat is on the loose. As their investigation progresses, the agents learn the victim was recently found guilty of placing a camera in the female changing rooms of the USS Camden, earning himself plenty of enemies - but did any of them hate him enough to kill?

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A man admits to killing his father and half-brother, but Goren and Eames discover he had been heavily influenced by a self-improvement guru. The detectives are forced to take an unorthodox approach to discover whether the chief suspect was coerced into carrying out the murders

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: As the investigation of Gary Munson goes on, his union takes action and threatens the lives in the SVU

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

As the SVU works with the joint-terrorism task force to find the suspects and the motive, Benson has to convince Barba to address allegations of rape and torture the boy's mother discloses

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent

programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Ramsbottom in Greater Manchester to lend her expertise to Charlotte Belcher, who has been running the 14-bedroom Grant Arms Hotel for two and a half years. Despite its attractive, central location, the 19th-century listed venue has developed a bad reputation as a hang-out for a 'rough crowd' - particularly at the bar. With no profits to pay for renovations, Charlotte has enlisted her husband and two children to help turn the Grant Arms around, but her work-life balance is starting to tip drastically away from home, and the beleaguered hotelier hopes Alex will find a way to boost the hotel's income while also restoring her private life

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: The boys let Tiki Pu's parrot escape after it promises to take them to hidden treasure - but instead, the bird leads them into the clutches of a band of monkey pirates

programme.title: Tashi
programme.desc: Jack and Tashi are chased by a giant bat while flying in their glider, and end up crashing in the forest - where they find a gang of bandits up to no good

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Martin and Pretty Wing freeze their wings and find themselves stuck in a crevasse

programme.title: Nils Holgersson
programme.desc: Nils persuades Martin to impress Soft Feather by doing an aerobatics demonstration

programme.desc: The adventures of a young boy and his pet kangaroo

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Homeowners work to increase the value of their properties, redesigning and refurbishing for maximum profits

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Amanda Lamb helps Michael Grant search for a holiday home in Fuerteventura on the Canary Islands for £60,000. Properties on offer include a bargain flat, a traditional farmhouse and rural accommodation in the desert

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A journey along the Thames from the Cotswold Hills to the North Sea, concentrating on the lives of the water voles, swans and eels that call it home, and highlighting the escalating battle to control the waterways despite technological advances in flood management. As London continues to sink faster than Venice, the untamed nature of the river is revealed

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: A cross-border collie suffers a setback when a disc from its spine blows and Noel Fitzpatrick designs a special bolt to substitute it. A four-year-old cat is taken to the surgery after being hit by a car, and it is bad news for the owners when the X-rays reveal its pelvis is badly damaged and the operation may cost thousands of pounds

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring and revealing lesser known areas of London for locals and tourists. Featuring Woolwich, Deptford and Lewisham. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Passport to Pimlico (1949)
programme.desc: The residents of a London suburb discover documents proving their neighbourhood is not part of Britain, and declare independence. Appointing their own head of state, they begin erecting borders as the government resorts to desperate measures to bring the community back under British rule. Ealing comedy, starring Stanley Holloway and Margaret Rutherford

programme.title: In the Doghouse (1961)
programme.desc: A newly qualified vet struggles to compete against an unscrupulous rival. Comedy, starring Leslie Phillips, Peggy Cummins, Hattie Jacques and James Booth

programme.title: Mantovani TV Specials
programme.desc: Mantovani's 1950s TV series featuring special guests and hits of the time played by Mantovani and his Orchestra

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb helps Deborah Robertson find a holiday home on the Caribbean island of Tobago, checking out a £149,000 hillside retreat and a traditional dwelling

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Having overcome a bout of health problems, septuagenarians Ena and John Ryman are keen to live a little, and that means selling their Guildford home and searching for a property on the Jurassic Coast. With a budget of up to £500,000, Seetha Hallett shows them round an unusual upside down thatch in Lyme Regis and a restored Georgian apartment in Sidmouth

programme.title: Hitler on Trial: The Story Behind the Film
programme.desc: Documentary about the Jewish lawyer Hans Litten who put Adolf Hitler on the witness stand in 1931, in the hopes of exposing the truth about his dangerous character

programme.title: Once a Soldier
programme.desc: Open for over 300 years and home to 320 old soldiers, the Royal Hospital in Chelsea is under threat. This series follows the residents for a year of their lives

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: As the destruction caused by Hitler's V1s in London and the south of England reached critical proportions, a new fighter was introduced to challenge the enemy's flying bombs. Wing Commander 'Bee' Beaumont remembers his first encounters with the Tempest, and the Wilkinson family from Croydon share their account of life on the ground with help from their day-to-day diary

programme.title: Murder Investigation Team
programme.desc: The discovery of a child's partially dismembered body on a Thames refuse barge leads the team into a painstaking search through tons of rubbish in search of clues - and a gruesome revelation during the post-mortem suggests the boy was the victim of a ritual sacrifice. Gritty drama, starring Lindsey Coulson

programme.title: IBA Boxing
programme.desc: IBA Boxing brings you the best of the adrenaline pumping fights and knock out action from their legendary events.

programme.title: British Gangsters: Faces of the Underworld
programme.desc: Bernard O'Mahoney explores the media's influence on building male celebrities

programme.title: Jasper Jones (2017)
programme.desc: When a town outcast suspected of murder shows 14-year-old Charlie Bucktin a dead body, the teenager attempts to find the killer. Mystery thriller, starring Levi Miller, Aaron McGrath and Angourie Rice

programme.title: 198 Grand (2019)
programme.desc: Three hapless criminals from South London get mixed up in an underworld double-cross, and find themselves in possession of a large sum of money that puts their lives in danger. Crime drama, starring Andy Frith and Dean Kilbey

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: Morale hits a new low following the previous day's poll as the coach arrives in Pisa. The tourists take snaps of the famous leaning tower and visit a cheese factory in Parma before decision time causes tempers to flare once again

programme.title: Black Island (1978)
programme.desc: Two boys become separated from the rest of their class while on a trip to a small island - and end up getting captured by a gang of armed criminals. Adventure, starring Michael Elphick, Martin Murphy and Michael Salmon

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring and revealing New Cross, Eltham, and Catford.

programme.title: The Face at the Window (1939)
programme.desc: In 1880, Paris is terrorised by a hideous murderer. An innocent man is accused of being the killer by a respected aristocrat who longs to get him out of the way so he can seduce his fiancee - but may also have other reasons for wanting a scapegoat. Horror, starring Tod Slaughter and John Warwick

programme.title: Where Angels Fear to Tread (1991)
programme.desc: A wealthy widow travels to Italy to escape the pressure of her in-laws, and promptly falls in love with a poor local. When the revived woman agrees to marry her foreign friend, her relatives back home are shocked by the apparent disregard for class, culture and their family's image, and set about putting an end to the unlikely union. Period drama, based on the novel by EM Forster, starring Helena Bonham Carter, Judy Davis and Rupert Graves

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Physiotherapist Farah and secondary school teacher Steve try to complete a series of increasingly difficult challenges, ranging from agility tests to skill trials, under pressure to hold their nerve as friends, family and the studio audience watch their every move. The further they go in the competition, the more money they win, up to a jackpot of £250,000. Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present another celebrity edition of the unorthodox quiz show, this time inviting well-known journalists to guess the least-popular correct answers to questions posed to members of the public. Competing are Jane Moore and Kevin O'Sullivan, Miranda Sawyer and Tony Parsons, Toby Young and Melissa Kite, and Lizo Mzimba and Frank Gardner

programme.title: Live Grand Slam of Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session from day one, featuring eight group matches at Ricoh Arena in Coventry. The tournament was won last year by Gerwyn Price who defeated Peter Wright 16-6 in the final. That win meant that Price defended the title he won back in 2018, after he beat Gary Anderson 16-13 in a highly controversial final. The tournament will be held behind closed doors due to the ongoing coronavirus pandemic.

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day four of the First Test at Trent Bridge, where the second innings was likely to be taking shape ahead of the final day. When England last hosted Australia on this ground in this format in 2005 they claimed a crucial three-wicket victory that gave them a 2-1 lead in the series heading into the final Test at the Oval, a lead that they did not relinquish as they triumphed in an Ashes series for the first time since 1987

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from the fifth and final day of the Third Test at Old Trafford, where the five-match series moved a step closer to completion. The sides last faced each other here in the Test arena in 2005 in a thrilling encounter that saw Australia defy the odds on the final day to secure a draw, with Brett Lee and Glenn McGrath surviving the final 24 balls after Ricky Ponting's man-of-the-match-winning innings of 156 had kept the England attack at bay

programme.title: The Ashes - Verdict
programme.desc: A panel of experts offer their analysis of the fifth day of the First Test between England and Australia at Trent Bridge, as the opening contest in the five-match series concluded

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day five of the First Test at Trent Bridge, where the opening contest of the five-match series was brought to a conclusion. Having decided to replace their head coach Mickey Arthur with former batsman Darren Lehmann just 16 days prior to the commencement of this series, Australia will have hoped to claim an unlikely victory in this encounter that could spur them on in the coming Test matches

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium, where the winner of the first major trophy of the 2011/12 season was decided. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset

programme.title: Live Grand Slam of Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session from day one, featuring eight group matches at Ricoh Arena in Coventry. The tournament was won last year by Gerwyn Price who defeated Peter Wright 16-6 in the final. That win meant that Price defended the title he won back in 2018, after he beat Gary Anderson 16-13 in a highly controversial final. The tournament will be held behind closed doors due to the ongoing coronavirus pandemic.
